4. Once the Eulerian path is found, the sequence is constructed by taking the first part of each edge. For example, each edge represents adding a digit, so the sequence is built by the starting node's prefix plus all the digits added along the path.

            // So, the code needs to process the digits in reverse order.

            // So, the code outline is:

            public String crackSafe(int n, int k) {
                if (n == 0) return ""; // though n >=1 per constraints.

                String initial = "";
                for (int i = 0; i < n-1; i++) {
                    initial += '0';
                }

                Map<String, List<Character>> graph = new HashMap<>();
                // Initialize the graph: for each possible node, add all possible next digits in reverse order.
                // But generating all possible nodes is tricky. Instead, perhaps during the DFS, nodes are created as needed.

                List<Character> path = new ArrayList<>();
                dfs(initial, graph, path, n, k);

                Collections.reverse(path);
                StringBuilder result = new StringBuilder();
                if (n > 1) {
                    result.append(initial);
                }
                for (char c : path) {
                    result.append(c);
                }
                if (n > 1) {
                    result.append(initial.substring(0, n-1));
                }

                return result.toString();
            }

            private void dfs(String node, Map<String, List<Character>> graph, List<Character> path, int n, int k) {
                if (!graph.containsKey(node)) {
                    graph.put(node, new ArrayList<>());
                    // Initialize the next digits in reverse order.
                    for (int i = k-1; i >=0; i--) {
                        char c = (char) ('0' + i);
                        graph.get(node).add(c);
                    }
                }

                List<Character> nextDigits = graph.get(node);
                while (!nextDigits.isEmpty()) {
                    char c = nextDigits.remove(nextDigits.size()-1); // take the last element (since we added in reverse order)
                    String nextNode = (node + c).substring(1); // for n>1, this is the new node.
                    dfs(nextNode, graph, path, n, k);
                    path.add(c);
                }
            }

            // So, dfs is called with node "0".

            // In this call, node is "0", which is not in the graph. So initialize nextDigits as '1', '0'.

            // nextDigits is ['1', '0'].

            // take '0' (last element), remove it, nextNode is "0" + '0' → "00".substring(1) is "0".

            // So, dfs("0", ...) again.

            // This will loop indefinitely because the node "0" will keep being called, and nextDigits will always have '1' and '0'.

            // Wait, that's a problem. Because for n=1, the node is a string of length 0, and the next node is the substring(1) of ("" + c) → which is c.

            // So, for n=1, the next node is the digit itself.

            // So, in the dfs function, for n=1, the next node is c.

                Collections.reverse(path);
                StringBuilder result = new StringBuilder();
                if (n > 1) {
                    result.append(initial);
                }
                for (char c : path) {
                    result.append(c);
                }
                if (n > 1) {
                    result.append(initial.substring(0, n-1));
                }

                return result.toString();
            }

                        char c = (char) ('0' + i);
                        graph.get(node).add(c);
                    }
                }

                List<Character> nextDigits = graph.get(node);
                while (!nextDigits.isEmpty()) {
                    char c = nextDigits.remove(nextDigits.size()-1); // take the last element (since we added in reverse order)
                    String nextNode = (node + c).substring(1); // for n>1, this is the new node.
                    dfs(nextNode, graph, path, n, k);
                    path.add(c);
                }
            }
